metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend! There are plenty of exciting activities happening in and around London that are perfect for a curious 3-year-old. From free museum visits and outdoor adventures to captivating theatre shows and Easter-themed events, there's something for everyone to enjoy!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive museum with collections of toys, design and childhood. Mini Museum is sensory space for under 2s
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and sensory areas are great for toddlers. Free and close to Hackney.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farm animals, see gardens, and enjoy a slice of the countryside in the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and a relaxed atmosphere are perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Several buses go directly to the farm from Hackney. Check Citymapper for the best route.
transport.tip: Check for special events like lambing season.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.address: 383 Stratford High Street, Stratford, London, E15 4QZ
activities.description: Immersive play spaces based on children's stories. Includes the Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and a playground make this a great place for active toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford station. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to guarantee entry.

activities.title: Walthamstow Wetlands
activities.description: Explore a large urban nature reserve with reservoirs, walking trails, and bird hides.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor space, wildlife, and easy walking trails are great for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Tottenham Hale and walk to the Wetlands.
transport.tip: Bring a picnic to enjoy by the water.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals and gardens in the heart of Spitalfields.
activities.whyItWorks: Compact size makes it easy for toddlers to explore. Free and close to Hackney.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Shoreditch High Street and walk to the farm.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts
activities.whyItWorks: Activities cater to young children with arts, crafts, and animal interactions.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Vauxhall Station
transport.tip: Pack a lunch to enjoy in the picnic area.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore playgrounds and open spaces
activities.whyItWorks: Offers wide spaces, playgrounds, and fountains for toddler enjoyment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford
transport.tip: Check out the Tumbling Bay Playground for a climbing adventure.

activities.title: William Morris Gallery
activities.address: Lloyd Park, Forest Rd, London E17 4PP
activities.description: Visit Mini Morris: Birthday Band and create a colourful tambourine.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on activities for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Walthamstow Central
transport.tip: Check out the park as well as the gallery.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur skeletons, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays. Easter Family Festival from April 5 to April 20.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and plenty of space to wander are great for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds. Check out the dinosaur gallery!

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Hands-on exhibits, space exploration gallery, and Wonderlab (paid).
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays engage toddlers. The Garden is a dedicated space for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: The Garden area is perfect for under 5s. Book Wonderlab tickets in advance.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Natural history museum with aquarium, butterfly house, and gardens. Mini Golf by UrbanCrazy Tickets not on sale
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of attractions, gardens, and animals make this a great day out for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station.
transport.tip: Check out the aquarium and butterfly house!

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo with rare birds and animals, plus a playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, animals, and a playground make this a fun and affordable day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Northern Line to Golders Green station.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world history and culture with fascinating artifacts.
activities.whyItWorks: Amazing exhibits spark curiosity. Pick up an Explorer Backpack for added engagement.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road station.
transport.tip: Visit the Egyptian exhibit and look for family trails.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A large playground with a Peter Pan theme, including a pirate ship and sensory trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Large, themed playground with lots of space to play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Queensway station.
transport.tip: Get there early as it can get crowded.

activities.title: Changing of the Guard
activities.description: A traditional ceremony with marching soldiers and music.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stimulating and a classic London experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube: Victoria Line to Green Park station.
transport.tip: Check the schedule in advance. Arrive early to get a good view.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.address: King William Walk, Greenwich, London SE10 9HT
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and historical, with a chocolate treat at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark station
transport.tip: Ideal for young explorers and their families.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.address: Hampton Court, East Molesey, Surrey KT8 9AU
activities.description: Follow the trail and find Lindt Gold Bunny statues hidden in the gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor fun, chocolate, and beautiful gardens make this a hit with toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train from Liverpool Street Station to Hampton Court Station.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance, especially for weekends.

activities.title: Easter Egg-Stravaganza
activities.description: Live show, egg hunt, easter bonnet parade, face painting, arts and crafts, and treats.
activities.whyItWorks: Activities for toddler with lots of variety.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to East Putney station, then walk or bus to the hospital.
transport.tip: Check the website for timings of different activities.

activities.title: Family Fun Day: Spectacular Space!
activities.description: Family Fun Day themed on Space
activities.whyItWorks: Space themed day.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Oxford Circus Station
transport.tip: Check for specific age-appropriate activities

activities.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
activities.description: Musical stage version of Michael Rosen's beloved book
activities.whyItWorks: Audience participation, and live music.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Wimbledon
transport.tip: Check out the cafe and buggy park.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Imaginative playtime and toys.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to London Bridge station
transport.tip: Bring your favourite toy.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Extraordinary puppets and dazzling stagecraft help bring Lewis Carroll's classic to life.
activities.whyItWorks: A classic tale with puppets.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hammersmith Station
transport.tip: Consider a matinee performance.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs and answer questions along the way to earn a delicious chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor exploration and chocolate rewards.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Eltham station
transport.tip: Enjoy the beautiful gardens and historic palace.

activities.title: Tower Bridge Family Activities
activities.dates: Tuesday 31 March, Tuesday 7 April and Saturday 11 April 2026
activities.price: Included in your visit to Tower Bridge
activities.description: Discover phenakistoscopes, the original animations that came before cartoons and GIFs. Create your own phenakistoscope of another Victorian icon – Tower Bridge!
activities.whyItWorks: Combines making, movement and mechanical marvels, giving children the chance to discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world, all while exploring the Bridge from top to bottom.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Tower Hill station
transport.tip: Drop-in throughout the day.
